interesting_fact: Japan has more than 6,800 islands and is home to the world’s oldest company, founded in 578 AD.
Canada has the longest coastline in the world — over 202,000 kilometers long.
Pakistan is home to the world’s highest paved international road — the Karakoram Highway — which connects Pakistan and China through the Himalayas.
Ancient Egyptians invented one of the earliest forms of writing — hieroglyphics.
France is the most visited country in the world, welcoming over 90 million tourists each year.
Australia is home to more kangaroos than humans.
India has a floating post office located on Dal Lake in Srinagar.
Norway introduced salmon sushi to Japan in the 1980s.
South Africa is the only country in the world with three capital cities.
Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the world.
Sri Lanka is known as the ‘Teardrop of India’ due to its unique shape and proximity to the Indian subcontinent.
Bangladesh is home to the Sundarbans, the largest mangrove forest in the world and the habitat of the Royal Bengal Tiger.
